Names may not be unique; if uniqueness is required, use professor_id instead.","overall_rating is an integer and non-null filter ensures we only average valid ratings."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"SELECT
  CONCAT(p.first_name, ' ', p.last_name) AS professor_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ce.overall_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_overall_rating
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.professors AS p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
WHERE ce.overall_rating IS NOT NULL
  AND cs.professor_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY professor_name ASC;","validationIssues":["Professor names may not be unique — consider including professor_id in the label if uniqueness is required.","The SQL orders by professor_name (alphabetical). visualization.title: Average Overall Course Evaluation Rating by Professor

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT
  CONCAT(p.first_name, ' ', p.last_name) AS professor_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ce.overall_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_overall_rating
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.professors AS p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
WHERE ce.overall_rating IS NOT NULL
  AND cs.professor_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY professor_name ASC;

issues: Professor names may not be unique — consider including professor_id in the label if uniqueness is required.
The SQL orders by professor_name (alphabetical). If the user expected professors ranked by average rating, ORDER BY avg_overall_rating DESC is needed.
If ratings use a known fixed scale (e.g., 1–5), consider setting the y-axis domain to that range for clearer interpretation.

visualization.title: Average Overall Course Evaluation Rating by Professor

result.sql:
SELECT
  CONCAT(p.first_name, ' ', p.last_name) AS professor_name,
  ROUND(AVG(ce.overall_rating)::numeric, 2) AS avg_overall_rating
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.professors AS p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
WHERE ce.overall_rating IS NOT NULL
  AND cs.professor_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY professor_name ASC;

issues: Professor names may not be unique — consider including professor_id in the label if uniqueness is required.
The SQL orders by professor_name (alphabetical). If the user expected professors ranked by average rating, ORDER BY avg_overall_rating DESC is needed.
If ratings use a known fixed scale (e.g., 1–5), consider setting the y-axis domain to that range for clearer interpretation.
